personality_voice.book_baseline: Jimmy Peakes is a short but broad-chested third-year Beater selected for Gryffindor. His only characterizing trait is physical capability - he hits Bludgers ferociously, hard enough to raise a lump on Harry's head. He also acts as a message-carrier in one scene, delivering Dumbledore's note to Harry.
personality_voice.film_portrayal: Jimmy Peakes does not appear in the film corpus at all.
personality_voice.difference: The character is entirely absent from the films.
personality_voice.penalty_logic: With zero film presence, no personality or voice can be evaluated. Score is 0 by default, not as a penalty for unfaithfulness but because there is nothing to compare.

narrative_role_agency.book_baseline: Jimmy serves two minor functions: he is selected as a Beater for the Gryffindor Quidditch team (a plot-relevant decision by Harry), and he delivers Dumbledore's summons to Harry, which triggers a key plot moment in HBP.
narrative_role_agency.film_portrayal: Jimmy Peakes does not appear in the film corpus.
narrative_role_agency.difference: Both functions - Beater selection and message delivery - are absent or handled differently in the films.
narrative_role_agency.penalty_logic: No film material exists to score. Score is 0 by default.

comparative.key_observations: Jimmy Peakes does not appear in the film corpus at all. All dimension scores are 0 not as a faithfulness penalty but because there is no film material to evaluate against the book baseline. Confidence is Low across all dimensions for the same reason. He is a very minor book character with no arc, minimal personality, and two small functional roles, so his absence from the films, while a complete omission, does not represent a significant adaptation loss in terms of characterization depth.
